name: eval-3-steelman-before-hardened
context_setup: User has run through 5 rounds of challenges on a thesis about switching to a 4-day work week. Hardness score is at 75. User says 'I think we're done, I've addressed everything.'
expected_behavior: Does NOT rubber-stamp the close at 75
Explicitly applies the steelman rule: no idea reaches 80+ without surviving a genuine steelman of the opposing view

Builds the strongest possible case AGAINST the 4-day work week (not a token objection — the real strongest argument, e.g., 'productivity research shows the claimed gains only appear in specific industries and knowledge-work contexts; in your context [X] the evidence is weaker')
Presents the steelman as a final round and asks the user to respond to it
Only after the user rebuts the steelman (or acknowledges the open edge) does the hardness score cross 80
If user cannot rebut the steelman: honestly holds the score at 75-79 and flags this as a remaining open item
Phase 5 (Closing): produces the final scorecard with all challenges and resolution status, summarizes the strongest version of the idea incorporating all refinements, lists any remaining open items
failure_modes_to_check: Rubber-stamps the close without applying the steelman rule
Inflates the score past 80 to match the user's 'I'm done' signal
Produces a weak steelman that the user can trivially dismiss
Skips the closing scorecard and summary
